assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md lines 26-29: 'Module structure - Every domain module follows a strict model/ + service/ + endpoints/ directory structure. Siblings examined: sbom/, advisory/, package/ under modules/fundamental/src/'. Lines 31-35: 'Error handling - All endpoint handlers return Result<T, AppError> using the AppError enum from common/src/error.rs. Errors are wrapped with .context("descriptive message") for contextual error chains'. Both patterns are explicitly identified with specific sibling file references.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 1.5 (lines 39-46): 'Would retrieve issue comments via jira.get_issue_comments(TC-9201) and search for comments starting with [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:. Result: No description digest found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced. Proceeding with warning rather than blocking execution, per the digest protocol.' conventions.md lines 14-17 also confirm this check.
